title: Git history-based file ranking service with priority computation
purpose: Analyzes git history to compute file touch counts, authorship, and priority scores for ranking files by importance.
module_context: Exports FileRanking and RankingOptions interfaces, a GENERATED_PATTERNS regex array, and functions to run ranking, spawn git log, parse git log output, and compute file rankings.

methods.name: runRanking
methods.normalized_params: (db: Database, project: string, opts: RankingOptions): Promise<number>

steps.content: Determine the time range for git log based on options or default to 90 days.
Spawn a git log process to collect commit history for the specified project.
IF the git log fails and is not an abort error, log a warning and return 0.
Parse the raw git log output into aggregated commit data per file.
Fetch all non-stub files from the database.
Prepare an update statement for modifying file records with ranking data.
Process each file row in a transaction, computing ranking and updating database records.
Return the count of updated files.

methods.name: spawnGitLog
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, sinceDays: number, signal?: AbortSignal): Promise<string>

steps.content: IF an abort signal is present and aborted, reject with an abort error.
Construct git log command arguments including all branches, file names, date range, and commit metadata format.
Spawn a child process to execute the git log command in the project directory.
Collect stdout and stderr data from the git log process.
IF an abort signal is received, terminate the git log process.
IF the git log process exits with an error, reject with the error message.
IF the process is aborted, reject with an abort error.
Resolve with the concatenated stdout data when the process completes successfully.
Initialize an empty map to store aggregated commit data per file.
IF the raw input is empty, return the empty map.
Split the raw input into lines for processing.
Iterate through lines to parse commit metadata and associated file changes.
IF an abort signal is received during processing, throw an abort error.
Parse each commit line to extract hash, timestamp, and author email.
For each file listed in a commit, update the aggregate data with commit hash, author, and timestamp.
Return the populated map of file aggregates.

methods.name: computeRanking
methods.normalized_params: (filePath: string, lineCount: number, aggregates: Map<string, RawAggregate>): FileRanking

steps.content: Retrieve the aggregated commit data for the specified file path.
Determine if the file is generated based on predefined patterns.
IF no aggregate data exists for the file, return a default ranking with zero values.
Identify the primary author by finding the author with the most commits to the file.
Identify co-authors who have made at least two commits to the file.
Calculate the touch count as the number of unique commits to the file.
Convert the last timestamp to an ISO date string for the last touched field.
Compute the priority score using touch count and line count with logarithmic scaling.
IF the file is generated, reduce the priority score by a factor of 0.1.
Return the computed ranking data for the file.
